strategic_alpha.reasoning: Compelling framing — browser-native agent computer removes setup friction. Trickle team credibility + 926 PH upvotes + runner-up AI Product of Year suggests real execution. But crowded sandbox market.

execution_grit.reasoning: Login required for all meaningful functionality. Free tier limited Claude Code access makes core claims hard to verify at T1 surface. Sandbox environment behind auth wall.

craft_and_soul.reasoning: Very high PH launch engagement (926 upvotes, runner-up AI Product of Year). Trickle team known for design quality. Live visual desktop and skill composability are UX-forward concepts.

value_signal.reasoning: Free tier exists but limited. Pro tier pricing not publicly listed (opacity penalty). Max at $200/mo is expensive — only viable for heavy users. Pricing transparency mixed.

claims.claim: Homepage (happycapy.ai) publicly accessible and product concept clear
claims.evidence: The homepage clearly states, "Happycapy - Agent-Native Computer for everyone" and describes the product concept as letting AI agents learn how you work.

claims.claim: Pricing page exists with free tier and Max ($200/mo) tier details
claims.evidence: The pricing page lists a Free tier at $0/month and a Max tier at $200/month.

claims.claim: Login/signup available for free tier access
Skills marketplace or skills catalog publicly visible
150+ AI models claim documented with model list
Live visual desktop / sandboxed execution demo available publicly
claims.evidence: You can see what the AI sees.

claims.claim: Agent teams feature described in public materials
Trickle team / founder credentials visible on site
claims.evidence: The corpus does not mention any team or founder credentials.

claims.claim: Pro tier pricing publicly stated (or confirmed opaque)
Open-source skills claim verifiable via GitHub repo
